Well rounded Full Stack Engineer (and a former CEO) who thrives in high pressure startup environments, mentor, geek and a budding ML/LLM researcher.

I'm an ML researcher @ MIT and a student at Northeastern University majoring in CS and Linguistics. I've built large-scale, efficient software for my neuromechanics and quantum photonics labs, for satellites, and for agentic video editing startups. I'm looking for co-ops (8 months full-time, Jan - August) in systems engineering, ML engineering and research, agentic systems, full-stack, GPU infra and programming, and embedded systems. My most recent projects: I built https://github.com/tetraslam/SHFLA and won the MIT Media Lab hackathon. It hit the front page of HN, and got me demo requests from Stephen Wolfram, David Sinclair, Joscha Bach, and Suno AI. I also built https://mosaic.so's developer API, 4 core tiles (intro, outro, watermark, and AI augment), universal media search (search over hundreds of thousands of videos/images/audio in <2s accurately), and social media agents which autonomously find, edit, and post content to great amounts of engagement (>10k impressions per post in testing)Location: San Francisco, CA / Boston, MARemote: NoWilling to relocate: Within the United States, Singapore, and Japan.Technologies: Python, Zig, C, C++, Langchain/Langgraph, Javascript, Linux, Supabase, Redis, Pytorch, Slurm, CUDA, HPC, FastAPI, Embedded Linux, Tinygrad, Concurrency, Triton, Bash, Typescript, NextJS, TailwindCSS, LangchainResume: https://tetraslam.world/resume.pdfWebsite: https://tetraslam.worldEmail: shresht@mit.edu

Hello there! My name is Julián and I'm passionate about building usable, understandable, scalable and secure abstractions for users and developers. I am a low-level software engineer with extensive (10+ years) experience shipping large, mission-critical, customer-facing software (Apple filesystems and kernel, WebKit) and I'm looking for a new challenge. I have strong history building dependable software in environments from startups to large corporations, and I value opportunities to work with new technologies and languages. Growing teams and mentoring junior engineers are also essential skills on my CV.I am detail-oriented (testing and code comments are crucial), and I value effective and kind communication. I bring a mindset focused on reliability even under ambiguous requirements, and I try to do the "right thing" even when it's difficult. Right now, as side projects I'm working on writing an emulator in Rust, and an app for some task planning.Feel free to send an email or LinkedIn message! I always welcome good conversation and interesting technical challenges.

At Pythagora (YC W24), I built Developer Relations 0 -> 1 from scratch as the first customer-facing hire on a 5-person team:* Launched and scaled all developer community channels (Discord, GitHub, X, LinkedIn, Reddit)* Wrote, updated, and improved all product documentation & READMEs* Shipped full-stack demo apps and wrote technical tutorials* Created YouTube videos and blog posts that grew our developer reach by 4×* Led marketing campaigns that drove tens of thousands of engineers to new product launches* Partnered with engineering and founders to shape the product roadmap through community feedbackBefore that, I worked at Streak (YC S11) for 7 years on their support team. I studied software engineering on the side. My GitHub includes both technical projects as well as examples of DevRel content I've created: https://github.com/westonludeke

Location: Detroit, MIRemote: YesWilling to relocate: noTechnologies: Embedded systems, automotive, machine learning, signal processing, data science, real time computing, safety processes (MISRA, ISO26262, etc), MATLAB, FPGA.Resume: LinkedIn -- https://www.linkedin.com/in/wcunningEmail: wdocunningham \@\ gmail.comBackground: Masters in control theory and signal processing, working in the auto industry for the last 7 years, first on low level powertrain software then the last year or so on LIDAR/computer vision for autonomous vehicles. I've done the most work in C, but I'm now contributing to safety development work with the Rust consortium and Ferrous Systems through my current employer. Recently had to read the Linux kernel source and use the BCC tool suite to find a subtle issue in the network stack causing high latency on sensor data for AV, also coincidentally fixed most of the network timing infrastructure.Currently doing software defined vehicle software architecture with a focus on fail-safe and fail-operational, loosely coupled, distributed systems. This includes some standards contributions and work with high level suppliers like Nvidia and Renesas for next-generation software/hardware system co-development.Primary skills include hard real time embedded systems, safety critical applications, firmware development, system integration and complex hardware/software interaction debugging, along with detailed data analysis problems.Side interests include machining, 3D printing, embedded system development, coffee roasting and furniture making.

Open to Staff+ roles at startups or scale-ups where infrastructure and system design are critical.
